title: Single-cell RNA-seq Metadata Schema [Darwin Core]
description: Schema for single-cell Ribonucleic Acid Sequencing (RNA-seq) studies focused on biodiversity-related information in alignment with the Darwin Core (DwC) standard. Version 0.4.

manifest.description: Manifest information about the schema itself.

study.description: Information about the study or project.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Title.description: A name given to the study or project. Project title should be fewer than 30 words.

Description.description: A detailed description of the project which includes research goals and experimental approach. Project description should be fewer than 300 words.

Bibliographic Citation.description: A citation for the study resource, following a standard format.

Created.description: The date when the study was created or registered.

Workflow (optional).description: The workflow or protocol followed during the study.

Technology.description: The sorting or visualisation technology used.

Licence (optional).description: Specifies the terms under which the data can be used, shared, or reused.

person.description: Information about people involved in the study.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Orcid ID (optional).description: A 16-digit number that uniquely identifies researchers.

First Name.description: A first name (or given name) is the personal name given to an individual conducting the study.

Last Name.description: A last name (or surname) is the family name passed down from one generation to the next.

Email Address (optional).description: A unique identifier used to send and receive electronic messages over the internet.

Affiliation or Institution.description: An organisation or institution that this person is associated with.

Funder (optional).description: A person or organization that supports (sponsors) something through some kind of financial contribution.

Grant Award (optional).description: A grant that directly or indirectly provides funding or sponsorship for the person to conduct the study.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the study if referring to.

Sample ID.description: A unique reference or identifier for the sample. This field must provide a consistent, unambiguous way to identify the sample within and across datasets.

Scientific Name or Organism (optional).description: The formal Latin name used to identify the organism from which the sample was derived.

Taxon ID.description: A unique identifier (usually from a recognized taxonomy database like NCBI Taxonomy) that corresponds to the organism's scientific name.

Biosample Accession.description: A unique identifier assigned to a biological sample after it has been submitted to a public database, such as the NCBI BioSample or ENA.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Dissociation Protocol ID.description: A unique alphanumeric code for the dissociation protocol in the study.

Protocol Name.description: A descriptive name of the protocol used for single-cell sequencing.

Dissociation Description.description: A free-text description of the process used to separate cells from tissues or cell aggregates.

Enrichment Markers (optional).description: Description of the specificity markers used to isolate cell populations, e.g. 'CD45+'.

Isolation Kit (optional).description: The kit used to isolate the cells.

Literature Source Reference (optional).description: Reference to literature sources that describe the protocol or methods used.

Protocols IO Reference (optional).description: Reference link to protocols.io for additional details on the protocol.

Workflowhub Sop Reference (optional).description: Reference to the Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) in workflow hub.

Dissociation Protocol Method (optional).description: The method used to dissociate tissues into single cells.

Single Cell Quality Metric (optional).description: Metrics used to assess the quality of single cells before sequencing.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Cell Suspension ID.description: A unique alphanumeric code for the cell suspension for the sample.

Sample ID.description: A unique reference or identifier for the sample associated with the cell suspension.

Dissociation Protocol ID.description: A unique alphanumeric code for the dissociation protocol in the study.

Suspension Type.description: The type of suspension used to keep cells in solution during processing.

Cell Count (optional).description: A number representing the number of cells in the sequencing library.

Cell Viability (optional).description: The percentage of living cells in a sample.

Cell Viability Assessment Method (option.description: The method used to evaluate the viability of cells in the sample.

Cell Size (optional).description: The size of the cell, typically measured in micrometres.

Suspension Volume (µL) (optional).description: The volume of the cell suspension in microlitres (µL).

Suspension Concentration Cells Per µL (o.description: The concentration of cells in the suspension in microlitres (µL).

Suspension Dilution (optional).description: The dilution factor of the cell suspension.

Loading Volume Μl (optional).description: The volume of the cell suspension loaded into the single-cell RNA-sequencing system.

Suspension Dilution Buffer (optional).description: A solution used to dilute cell suspensions to a desired concentration.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Library Preparation ID.description: A unique alphanumeric reference or identifier for the library preparation protocol.

Cell Suspension ID.description: A unique alphanumeric code for the cell suspension for the library preparation.

Library Preparation Kit.description: Packaged kits tailored for specific sequencing workflows.

Library Preparation Kit Version.description: The version number of the library preparation kit.

Amplification Method (optional).description: The method used to amplify the Complementary DNA (cDNA).

cDNA Amplification Cycles (optional).description: The number of cycles used during the cDNA amplification process.

Average Size Distribution (optional).description: The average length of RNA fragments in base pairs after library preparation.

Library Construction Method (optional).description: The library construction method (including version) that was used.

Input Molecule (optional).description: The specific fraction of biological macromolecule from which the sequencing library is derived.

Primer (optional).description: The type of primer used for reverse transcription.

Primeness.description: The end from which the molecule was sequenced.

End Bias (optional).description: The end bias of the library.

Library Strand (optional).description: The cDNA strand of the library from which the reads derived.

Spike In.description: External RNA added to the sample as a control. State whether spike-in was used.

Spike Type (optional).description: The specific type of external RNA used for spiking in.

Spike In Dilution Or Concentration (opti.description: The final concentration or dilution of the spike in mix.

i5 Index.description: Barcode sequence used on the i5 adapter during library preparation.

i7 Index.description: Barcode sequence used on the i7 adapter to distinguish samples.

Dual or Single Index.description: Specifies if both i5 and i7 indices or only one index was used.

I5 Sequence.description: The nucleotide sequence of the i5 index used in multiplexing during sequencing.

i7 Sequence.description: The specific nucleotide sequence of the i7 index used for a sample.

Plate ID (optional).description: Identifier for the 96-well plate used in sample preparation.

Well Row (optional).description: The row identifier in a 96-well plate indicating the sample's position.

Well Column (optional).description: The column identifier in a 96-well plate indicating the sample's position.

Cell Phenotype (optional).description: The cell marker for the Fluorescence-Activated Cell Sorting (FACS) of cells.

Design description (optional).description: The design of the library including details of how it was constructed.

Library selection.description: The method used to select for or against, enrich, or screen the material being sequenced.

Library source.description: The type of source material that is being sequenced.

Library strategy.description: The sequencing technique intended for this library.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Sequencing ID.description: A unique alphanumeric reference or identifier for the sequencing protocol.

Sequencing Platform Name.description: The name of the sequencing platform used for the experiment.

Sequencing Instrument Model.description: The machine or platform used for sequencing.

Library Layout.description: Specify whether to expect single, paired, or other configuration of reads.

UMI Barcode Read (optional).description: The type of read that contains the Unique Molecular Identifier (UMI) barcode.

UMI Barcode Offset (optional).description: The offset in sequence of the UMI identifying barcode.

UMI Barcode Size (optional).description: The size of the UMI identifying barcode.

Cell Barcode Read (optional).description: The type of read that contains the cell identifying barcode.

Cell Barcode Offset (optional).description: The offset in sequence of the cell identifying barcode.

Cell Barcode Size (optional).description: The size of the cell identifying barcode.

cDNA Read.description: The actual nucleotide sequence obtained from cDNA during sequencing.

cDNA Read Offset (optional).description: The starting position of the cDNA read within the entire sequence.

cDNA Read Size (optional).description: The size of the cDNA read.

analysis_derived_data.description: Information about data derived from analysis.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

File Derived From (optional).description: The name of the file that was used to generate the analysis derived data.

Inferred Cell Type (optional).description: Post analysis cell type or identity declaration based on expression profile or known gene function.

Post Analysis Cell Well Quality (optiona.description: Performer defined measure of whether the read output from the cell was included in the sequencing analysis.

Other Derived Cell Attributes (optional).description: Any other cell level measurement or annotation as result of the analysis.

raw_data_processing.description: Information about raw data processing steps.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Reference Genome (optional).description: Indicate version and include stable link to genome data.

Genome Annotation (optional).description: Indicate version and include stable link. Also indicate if any modification to the original annotation has been applied.

Genes vs Exons (optional).description: Quantification using whole gene intervals or exons.

Mapping and Demultiplexing Software (opt.description: Software used for mapping and demultiplexing.

Read Mapping Statistics (optional).description: Statistics of the Reads or Unique Molecular Identifier (UMI).

UMIs or Barcode Distribution QC (optiona.description: Show UMIs per barcode distribution and threshold applied.

Cell or Non-Cell Filtering Strategy (opt.description: UMI threshold used to discriminate cells from non-cells. Description of algorithm and parameters used.

Other Quality Filters Applied (optional).description: Cells/nuclei discarded based on % mitochondrial reads, % rRNA reads, etc.

Ambient RNA QC (optional).description: Report % UMIs in background cell barcodes, and algorithm used to remove ambient RNA.

Individual Organism SNP Demultiplexing (.description: If carried out, show SNP partitioning quality, algorithm used.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.
A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Source Code (optional).description: If any newly developed code/software has been used.

UMI Count Matrix (optional).description: Gene x cell matrix with UMI counts for each gene in each cell.

Ensembl IDs (optional).description: Gene or transcript names should be listed as Ensembl (or other standardized ID).

Protein Models (optional).description: FASTA file with the predicted proteins associated to genes.

Cell Metadata (optional).description: Table mapping cell IDs to cluster/cell type/broad cell type annotations.

Cluster-Level Normalised Expression Tabl.description: Expression tables that show normalised gene expression at the cluster or cell-type level.

Other Resource Files (optional).description: Necessary to re-use and interpret the data.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

File ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this file.

Library Preparation ID.description: A unique alphanumeric reference for the library preparation protocol.

Sequencing ID.description: A unique alphanumeric reference for the sequencing protocol.

Read 1 File.description: The name or accession of the file that contains read 1.

Read 2 File (optional).description: The name or accession of the file that contains read 2.

Index 1 File (optional).description: The name of the file that contains index 1.

Index 2 File (optional).description: The name of the file that contains index 2.

Read 1 Checksum.description: Result of a hash function calculated on the content of the read 1 file to verify file integrity.

Read 2 Checksum (optional).description: Result of a hash function calculated on the content of the read 2 file to verify file integrity.

White List Barcode File (optional).description: A file containing the known cell barcodes in the dataset.

expression_data_process_setting.description: Settings used for expression data processing.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

Expression Data Process Setting ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the expression data process setting.

Annotation Version (optional).description: The annotation version of the associated reference genome.

Dimensionality Reduction (optional).description: Method used to reduce dimensionality in the expression data.

Number of Nearest Neighbours (optional).description: Number of nearest neighbours used to calculate cluster membership.

Clustering Distance Metric (optional).description: Metric used to calculate a point's distance to others.

Software Versions (optional).description: Primary software packages used for analysis.

Cell Type Annotation (optional).description: Tools and Databases used for cell annotation.

Generated by Pipeline (optional).description: URL of the deposited pipeline used to create this data.

Study ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for this study.

File ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the expression data file.

Library Preparation ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for library preparation.

Expression Data Process Setting ID.description: A unique alphanumeric identifier for the expression data process setting.

File md5 Checkshum.description: Calculated md5 checksum for this file.

File Format.description: The format of the expression file, such as h5ad or rds.

Number of Cells (optional).description: The number of cells represented in the expression data.

Number of Genes (optional).description: The number of genes represented in the expression data.

File Size in Bytes (optional).description: Size of the file recorded in bytes.

Date Generated (optional).description: Approximate date this expression data was generated.
